original_abstract: This research evaluates the quality of the e-learning system at Universitas Mahakarya Asia by applying the McCall Method to assess the platform's overall performance. Focusing on the e-learning.unmaha.ac.id portal, the study examines six critical software quality dimensions: correctness, reliability, efficiency, integrity, usability, and maintainability. The evaluation yielded a cumulative quality score of 79.6%. While the results demonstrate high levels of reliability and data integrity, they also identify specific technical gaps, particularly concerning the accuracy of information and the complexity of system maintenance. Based on these findings, we propose a series of targeted improvements to refine content correctness, streamline the user interface, and simplify backend maintenance processes. By prioritizing these enhancements, the university can ensure the platform's long-term sustainability and provide a more responsive, high-quality digital learning environment for its students.
humanized_abstract: How good is an e-learning system at Universitas Mahakarya Asia? This research aimed to give an overall assessment using McCall method. With a focus on e-learning system through e-learning.unmaha.ac.id the authors evaluate the six basic characteristics of software quality, namely correctness, reliability, efficiency, integrity, usability, and maintainability and found that the cumulative quality score is 79.6%. Hence, there are strong proofs of the reliability and data integrity, but there are some technical weakness such as information correctness, system usability, etc. Thus, several solutions are recommended to enhance the learning system namely increasing information correctness, minimizing user interface, and minimizing the backend maintenance.
